economic_notes: Splatsin has equity investments in Yucwmenlucwu (Caretakers of the Land) 2007 LLP (logging and environmental services, 99% interest), Quilakwa Investments Ltd. (retail gas station and convenience store, 100%), Splatsin Development Corporation (100%), and BC First Nations Gaming Revenue Sharing LP (0.52%). Yucwmenlucwu revenues were $14,903,694 and net income $3,967,923 in 2021. Quilakwa revenues were $9,555,689 and net income $292,887. Multi-purpose community facility and gas bar/convenience store constructed on Enderby Indian Reserve #2, financed through FNFA for $18,259,622.
notes: Fiscal year ended March 31, 2021 (not 2024). Auditor changed from Daley & Company CPA LLP (prior year) to Grant Thornton LLP (current year) after the two firms merged in January 2021. Three entities consolidated using modified equity method: Quilakwa Investments Ltd., Splatsin Development Corporation, Yucwmenlucwu 2007 LLP. Sexqeltkemc Limited Partnership discontinued operations. CMHC Rapid Housing Initiative deferred revenue of $2,957,500 is a significant new item. Annual debt service figure reflects long-term debt reduction only; demand loan and mortgage repayments excluded. Wages and benefits line from segmented schedule was cut off in the source text. Remuneration schedule not included in provided text. OSR calculated as total revenue minus ISC, FNHA, and CMHC transfers ($10,124,992 in government transfers).
